body: recall applies a 0.35 relative score cutoff; raising it broke nothing but lowering it re-admits noise, tune only with bench/token_savings.py as the gate

Indexing walk is deliberately bounded to avoid pegging CPU on large trees: skip files larger than 512KB (MAX_FILE_BYTES), skip *.min.* minified assets, and honor an optional .limpetignore (gitignore syntax, applies even outside a git repo). Root cause that motivated this: pointing limpet at a full WordPress install (~9950 files, no .gitignore) walked wp-admin/wp-includes/vendor and fed multi-MB generated bundles to tree-sitter, pegging CPU with no useful anchors.

main.rs uses a hand-rolled arg parser, not clap. Help/version flags MUST be short-circuited at the top of run() before dispatch; otherwise `limpet index --help` falls through to the index arm and runs a full index of the current directory (the flag is silently ignored). Keep the -h/--help and -V/--version guard as the first thing run() does.
Full index of the limpet repo itself is fast: ~30 files, ~242 symbols, ~300ms. If a limpet call hangs, the walk hit an unbounded external tree (missing ignore rules), not a code bug in the indexer.

Hang RCA (2026-07): every limpet MCP call hung and 127.0.0.1:9748 refused connections because a `limpet index` process was pegged at 100% CPU indexing a 9950-file WordPress tree with no .gitignore. It never bound the port because it never finished the walk. Fix: kill the pegged proc (safe: Claude Code respawns the MCP server on next call, and the kill committed the pending WAL) and bound the walk (size cap + minified skip + .limpetignore).

Since v0.5.0 the index covers EVERY bounded file, not just symbol-bearing ones: grammar files (php/js/ts/py/rs) get symbols, everything else gets a file-level row (lang=NULL, raw-byte content hash). Full index of the limpet repo: 47 files / 275 symbols, <300ms. Hidden paths are walked (hidden(false)) so .github/workflows and .gitignore are anchorable; .git and .limpet dirs are blocklisted.

v0.5.0 anchor-trust rules: (1) remember validates ALL anchors before writing, in one transaction; any unresolved anchor = loud error, zero rows persisted (no phantom anchored counts, no orphan entries). (2) File-level anchors store the file content hash and go stale:file_edited on change, invalidated on delete; legacy NULL-hash anchors are backfilled on first resolve. (3) Per-anchor aggregation: entry is invalidated only when EVERY anchor dies; partial loss = stale:anchor_lost. Rejected alternative: worst-anchor-wins (v0.4.0) because one dead anchor nuked memories that were still mostly valid.

recall applies a 0.35 relative score cutoff; raising it broke nothing but lowering it re-admits noise, tune only with bench/token_savings.py as the gate. Still holds in v0.5.0 (whole-repo indexing did not move the bench).

Hang RCA (2026-07): every limpet MCP call hung and 127.0.0.1:9748 refused connections because a `limpet index` process was pegged at 100% CPU indexing a 9950-file WordPress tree with no .gitignore. It never bound the port because it never finished the walk. Fix: kill the pegged proc (safe: Claude Code respawns the MCP server on next call, and the kill committed the pending WAL) and bound the walk (size cap + minified skip + .limpetignore). Those bounds were kept verbatim when v0.5.0 moved to whole-repo file-level indexing; they are the CPU-peg defense, not the extension filter.

Publish pipeline gotchas (learned shipping 0.5.1): (1) mcp-registry workflow must trigger on tag push, NOT release-published: the GH release is created by the release workflow with GITHUB_TOKEN, and GitHub suppresses events from GITHUB_TOKEN-authored actions, so a release trigger never fires. (2) Registry namespace is case-sensitive: OIDC grants io.github.KSym04/* (capital KS), lowercase in server.json gets 403. (3) server.json description caps at 100 chars. (4) crates.io publish requires a verified email on the crates.io account; the token alone is not enough (400 at Uploading step).

Since v0.6.0 the index covers every bounded file with SIX grammars (php/js/ts/py/rs/cpp; cpp claims cpp/cc/cxx/hpp/hh/hxx/h/c/inl). Degradation ladder (invariant I-N1, a grammar only ever upgrades a file): valid UTF-8 under 512KB -> symbols; non-UTF-8 (CP949/UTF-16) or over 512KB (MAX_PARSE_BYTES) -> file-level row with raw-byte hash; over 8MB (MAX_FILE_BYTES) -> skipped by walker. Hidden paths walked; .git and .limpet blocklisted; store data dir excluded. Full index of the limpet repo: 48 files / 283 symbols / ~370ms.

Publish pipeline gotchas: (1) mcp-registry workflow triggers on tag push, NOT release-published: GitHub suppresses events from GITHUB_TOKEN-authored actions. (2) Registry namespace is case-sensitive: io.github.KSym04/* (capital KS); lowercase gets 403. (3) server.json description caps at 100 chars. (4) crates.io publish needs a verified account email; the token alone gets 400 at upload. (5) gh run rerun refuses while the Intel mac job keeps the run in_progress; publish locally instead of waiting.
main.rs uses a hand-rolled arg parser, not clap. Help/version flags MUST be matched before the subcommand dispatch; adding a new subcommand means updating both the match and the HELP string.

recall applies a 0.35 relative score cutoff (floor = top * 0.35 in recall.rs); raising it broke nothing but lowering it re-admits noise. Tune only with bench/token_savings.py as the gate. Still holds in v0.6.0.

Hang RCA (2026-07): every limpet MCP call hung and 127.0.0.1:9748 refused connections because a limpet index process was pegged at 100% CPU indexing a 9950-file WordPress tree with no .gitignore. Fix: kill the pegged proc and bound the walk. Bounds evolved in v0.6.0 into a degradation ladder (512KB parse cap -> file-level, 8MB walk skip) but remain the CPU-peg defense.

limpet update atomically replaces the binary on disk but a RUNNING serve process keeps the old code image. Two servers on one store = old-code resolver racing new-code writes; observed one spurious invalidation (file anchors written hashless by an old image, then judged by mixed code). After every update, kill lingering limpet serve processes or fully restart Claude Code. Robustness candidate for v0.6.1: version-stamp the store and have stale-image servers refuse to resolve.

v0.6.1 ships the store version guard (issue #9): Store::version_guard runs first in every tools::dispatch call and before CLI index. It stamps code_version into meta_kv, upgrades older stamps, and a process OLDER than the stamp gets a loud refusal naming both versions. Reads are not exempted because every tool call sweeps+resolves (writes). Limitation: images <= 0.6.0 predate the guard and cannot refuse; protection covers all future image mixes.
evidence_cmd: LIMPET_DATA_DIR=... limpet index on a 99.0.0-stamped store

body: Publish pipeline gotchas: (1) mcp-registry workflow triggers on tag push, NOT release-published: GitHub suppresses events from GITHUB_TOKEN-authored actions. (2) Registry namespace is case-sensitive: io.github.KSym04/* (capital KS); lowercase gets 403. (3) server.json description caps at 100 chars. (4) crates.io publish needs a verified account email; token alone gets 400. (5) gh run rerun refuses while the Intel mac job keeps a run in_progress; publish locally instead.

main.rs uses a hand-rolled arg parser, not clap. Help/version flags MUST be matched before the subcommand dispatch; adding a new subcommand means updating both the match and the HELP string. Since 0.6.1 the index arm also calls store.version_guard() before full_index.

limpet update replaces the binary on disk but a RUNNING serve process keeps the old code image; two images on one store caused a spurious invalidation (2026-07-03). FIXED in 0.6.1: Store::version_guard stamps the store and stale-newer images get a loud refusal. Residual risk: images <= 0.6.0 predate the guard and still write blind; after updating FROM an old version, kill lingering limpet serve processes manually.

v0.7.0 ships the savings ledger: recall_cost prices every recall (served = packer-identical counting; baseline = 300-token search overhead + distinct anchored files at ceil(size/4); negatives never floored; anchorless = zero baseline). Counters in meta_kv behind the version guard. Surfaced ONLY where humans read free: admin op=ledger, limpet stats, ui /api/ledger. NOT on the recall wire: a 2-int block dropped the bench 4.1x -> 3.9x under its own 4x gate and was removed.

recall applies a 0.35 relative score cutoff (floor = top * 0.35); raising it broke nothing but lowering it re-admits noise. Tune only with bench/token_savings.py as the gate. Held through v0.7.0 (ledger instrumentation did not move the bench; a wire-side ledger block DID, and was removed).

Publish pipeline gotchas: (1) mcp-registry triggers on tag push, NOT release-published (GITHUB_TOKEN events suppressed). (2) Registry namespace case-sensitive: io.github.KSym04/*. (3) server.json description <= 100 chars. (4) crates.io needs verified account email. (5) gh run rerun refuses while any job in a run is live; publish locally instead.

main.rs uses a hand-rolled arg parser, not clap. Help/version flags MUST be matched before subcommand dispatch; a new subcommand means updating both the match and HELP. index and stats arms call store.version_guard() before touching the store.

Publish pipeline gotchas: (1) mcp-registry triggers on tag push, NOT release-published (GITHUB_TOKEN events suppressed). (2) Registry namespace case-sensitive: io.github.KSym04/*. (3) server.json description <= 100 chars, name and version must sync with Cargo.toml each release. (4) crates.io needs verified account email. (5) gh run rerun refuses while any job is live. Deliberately NOT anchored to server.json: version bumps edit it every release and would stale this memory with zero knowledge change.

v0.7.2 hardening semantics (from the 2026-07 three-audit pass): invalidation HEALS when code returns (only superseded is final); file anchors follow moves by content hash; stale confidence penalty applies once on transition; recall relevance floor exempts flagged items and skips non-positive tops; C++ renames follow via declarator-chain name exclusion; remember refuses verified-without-evidence and ambiguous bare names; index_file/version_guard/ledger_add transactional; limpet doctor ships and auto-runs after install/update (auto-run visible from the NEXT update, the running 0.7.1 image predated it).

Anchor-trust rules as of 0.7.2: remember validates ALL anchors in one tx (loud failure, zero rows); verified requires evidence; ambiguous bare names refused with candidate FQNs; anchor files reindexed pre-hash. Resolution: entry invalidated only when EVERY anchor dies, partial loss = stale:anchor_lost, and invalidation HEALS if code returns (branch switch/stash are not death; only superseded is final). File anchors hash content, follow moves by hash. Stale confidence penalty applies once, on transition.

Index coverage as of 0.7.2: six grammars (php/js/ts/py/rs/cpp), every bounded file indexed; degradation ladder = symbols -> file-level (non-UTF-8 or >512KB) -> skipped (>8MB). index_file is fully transactional (no half-indexed files) and parses each file ONCE for all symbol hashes. Hidden paths walked; .git/.limpet/store data dir excluded.

Store version guard (0.6.1, atomic since 0.7.2): compare-and-stamp runs in one IMMEDIATE transaction; a process older than the stamp gets a loud refusal naming both versions before any write, including sweep/resolve. Limitation unchanged: images <= 0.6.0 predate the guard.

limpet update replaces the binary on disk but a RUNNING serve keeps the old image; caused one spurious invalidation (2026-07-03), fixed by the version guard. Post-0.7.2 the updater also runs limpet doctor automatically after updating (visible from the NEXT update; the running old image predates the feature) and stages the new binary next to the target with create_new instead of a predictable temp path.

Savings ledger (0.7.0, hardened 0.7.2): recall_cost prices every recall (served = packer-identical; baseline = 300-token search overhead + distinct anchored files at ceil(size/4); negatives never floored; anchorless = zero baseline). ledger_add is IMMEDIATE-transactional; the session base is in-memory per process (a shared-kv base let one boot clobber another's session view). Surfaced only where humans read free: admin op=ledger, limpet stats, ui /api/ledger.

recall relevance floor: floor = top * 0.35, but since 0.7.2 flagged items (stale/contradicted) are EXEMPT (I3 must hold: nothing flagged is ever score-hidden) and the floor is skipped when top <= 0 (multiplicative floors invert on negatives). Tune only with bench/token_savings.py as the gate; honesty exemption costs ~0.1x, bench gate holds at 4.0x.

main.rs uses a hand-rolled arg parser, not clap. Help/version flags MUST be matched before subcommand dispatch; a new subcommand means updating both the match and HELP. index/stats arms call store.version_guard() first; doctor is the diagnostics arm (doctor_run shared with post-install/post-update auto-checks).

0.7.3 security+Windows hardening (two-audit pass, cargo-audit clean 123 deps): import_jsonl now enforces remember's guards on untrusted teammate JSONL (secrets rejected, future updated_at rejected, 1MiB line cap, confidence clamped, 64KiB body cap, anchor hashes RE-RESOLVED against local index so a forged hash cannot fake freshness); ImportReport.rejected counts refusals. Windows root cause fixed: canonicalize() yields \?\ verbatim paths that break the /-keyed index for subdirs (CI missed it, tests use TempDir); util::canonicalize_plain strips the prefix, normalize_rel converts \ to / at every tool boundary. Updater download capped 128MiB; secret detector splits @ and /; validate_rel_path rejects reserved device names.
evidence_cmd: cargo test --locked; cargo audit; import-secret dogfood; windows-latest CI

body: Anchor resolution as of 0.7.3 (resolve_all): entry invalidated only when EVERY anchor dies; partial loss = stale:anchor_lost. Invalidation HEALS: invalidated entries are re-resolved, so a branch switch/stash/rebase that briefly removes files recovers when they return; only superseded is final. Symbol anchors follow renames/moves by body hash; file anchors follow moves by content hash. Stale confidence penalty applies ONCE on the active->stale transition (CASE reads pre-update status). (fqn,hash) existence check stops duplicate-FQN flapping. C++ names resolved through the declarator chain and excluded from the hash so C++ renames follow.

Write-path guards as of 0.7.3, enforced on BOTH remember and import (import is untrusted teammate JSONL): secrets rejected via secrets::detect on body+evidence; body capped at MAX_BODY_BYTES (64KiB); confidence clamped [0,1]; verified source requires evidence; ambiguous bare-name anchors refused with candidate FQNs; anchor files reindexed pre-hash so no anchor is born stale. Import additionally: 1MiB per-line cap, future updated_at rejected (LWW poison), imported anchor hashes RE-RESOLVED against local index so a forged hash cannot fake freshness. ImportReport.rejected counts refusals. remember is atomic (one tx, zero rows on any failure).

limpet update replaces the binary on disk but a RUNNING serve keeps the old image; caused one spurious invalidation (2026-07-03), fixed by the store version guard (0.6.1). 0.7.x additions: updater stages the new binary next to the target with create_new (not a predictable temp path) and caps the download at 128MiB; after updating it runs limpet doctor automatically. That post-update doctor runs in the PRE-SWAP image, so 0.7.3 labels its version/store lines as pre-restart instead of printing a confusingly stale number (doctor_run takes a post_update flag).

main.rs uses a hand-rolled arg parser, not clap. Help/version flags matched before subcommand dispatch; a new subcommand updates both the match and HELP. Commands: serve/ui/index/status/stats/doctor/export/import/install/uninstall/update. root_from uses util::canonicalize_plain (strips the Windows \\?\ verbatim prefix that otherwise breaks the /-keyed index for subdirs). index/stats call version_guard first. doctor_run(post_update: bool) is shared by the doctor command and the auto-checks after install/update. tests/docs_in_sync.rs fails the build if a tool/grammar/command is undocumented in the README.
